name: feat-received-exception-stack-trace
title: Add stack trace persistence and display for failed received messages
description: Add a dedicated ExceptionInfo column to the Received table, capture ex.ToString() on subscriber failure, and display it in a dedicated Exception tab in the MessageDetailDialog.

userStories.title: Add ExceptionInfo property to MediumMessage [XS]
userStories.description:
TDD order: write failing test → implement minimal code → verify PASS

Add a nullable ExceptionInfo string property to MediumMessage. MemoryMessage extends MediumMessage so it inherits the property automatically.
acceptanceCriteria: MediumMessage has a public `string? ExceptionInfo { get; set; }` property
InMemory storage's MemoryMessage (if it exists as a subclass) inherits the property without additional changes

userStories.title: Add ExceptionInfo column to database schemas [M]
userStories.description:
Sequential: update CREATE TABLE DDL → add ALTER TABLE for upgrades → update MERGE/INSERT statements → verify

Add ExceptionInfo TEXT NULL column to Received table in both SqlServer and PostgreSql initializers. Must be idempotent for both fresh installs and upgrades. Update _StoreReceivedMessage MERGE/INSERT to include ExceptionInfo. Update StoreReceivedExceptionMessageAsync implementations to accept and persist exceptionInfo.
acceptanceCriteria: SqlServer CREATE TABLE includes ExceptionInfo NVARCHAR(MAX) NULL as the last column before MessageId or after it
SqlServer ALTER TABLE with IF NOT EXISTS guard adds the column for existing databases
PostgreSql CREATE TABLE includes ExceptionInfo TEXT NULL
PostgreSql ALTER TABLE ADD COLUMN IF NOT EXISTS for existing databases
IDataStorage.StoreReceivedExceptionMessageAsync signature extended with string? exceptionInfo = null (backward-compatible default)
All 3 StoreReceivedExceptionMessageAsync implementations persist exceptionInfo to storage
_StoreReceivedMessage MERGE/INSERT in SqlServer and PostgreSql include ExceptionInfo in both UPDATE and INSERT branches

userStories.title: Capture and persist exception info in failure paths [M]
userStories.description:
TDD order: write failing test → implement minimal code → verify PASS

Capture ex.ToString() in both failure paths and persist via storage. Path A (deserialization failure in IConsumerRegister): declare exceptionInfo variable before try, set in catch, forward to StoreReceivedExceptionMessageAsync. Path B (subscriber execution failure in SubscribeExecutor._SetFailedState): set message.ExceptionInfo = ex.ToString() before calling ChangeReceiveStateAsync. Split _ChangeMessageStateAsync in SqlServer and PostgreSql so ChangeReceiveStateAsync includes ExceptionInfo in UPDATE SQL while ChangePublishStateAsync keeps the existing SQL.
acceptanceCriteria: _SetFailedState sets message.ExceptionInfo = ex.ToString() before calling ChangeReceiveStateAsync
IConsumerRegister catch block captures e.ToString() and forwards it to StoreReceivedExceptionMessageAsync
SqlServer ChangeReceiveStateAsync uses separate SQL that includes ExceptionInfo in UPDATE
PostgreSql ChangeReceiveStateAsync uses separate SQL that includes ExceptionInfo in UPDATE
InMemory ChangeReceiveStateAsync sets ExceptionInfo on the stored MemoryMessage
ChangePublishStateAsync is unaffected (no ExceptionInfo in Published table)

userStories.title: Update monitoring APIs to read ExceptionInfo [S]
userStories.description:
Sequential: update SELECT → update reader mapping → verify

Update _GetMessageAsync in SqlServer and PostgreSql monitoring APIs to include ExceptionInfo in the SELECT query and map it to MediumMessage.ExceptionInfo. InMemory already has it in memory.
acceptanceCriteria: SqlServer _GetMessageAsync SELECT includes ExceptionInfo and maps it to MediumMessage.ExceptionInfo
PostgreSql _GetMessageAsync SELECT includes ExceptionInfo and maps it to MediumMessage.ExceptionInfo
InMemory GetReceivedMessageAsync returns ExceptionInfo (already available on MemoryMessage)

userStories.title: Update dashboard endpoint to return ExceptionInfo [XS]
userStories.description:
Sequential: update endpoint → verify API response

Add ExceptionInfo to the anonymous object returned by _ReceivedMessageDetails endpoint. Published message detail endpoint is unaffected.
acceptanceCriteria: _ReceivedMessageDetails returns ExceptionInfo = message.ExceptionInfo in the JSON response
_PublishedMessageDetails response is unchanged

userStories.title: Add Exception tab to MessageDetailDialog [M]
userStories.description:
Sequential: update TypeScript interface → add tab UI → test typecheck → verify

Add exceptionInfo field to the MessageDetail TypeScript interface. When exceptionInfo is present and non-empty, show a dedicated 'Exception' tab alongside the existing 'Content' tab using v-tabs. The Exception tab renders the full ex.ToString() output as pre-formatted text. Remove or repurpose the existing dead exception detection code (buildContentKind/isExceptionObject). All changes must pass vue-tsc --build.
acceptanceCriteria: MessageDetail interface includes exceptionInfo?: string
When exceptionInfo is present, a v-tabs UI shows 'Content' and 'Exception' tabs
Exception tab renders exceptionInfo as pre-formatted monospace text with the existing .stack-trace-code styling
When exceptionInfo is absent/empty, no tabs — content displays as before (backward compatible)
vue-tsc --build passes without errors

findings: No migration framework — schema managed by idempotent DDL in IStorageInitializer.InitializeAsync()
Exception currently stored as 'Type-->Message' in Headers['headless-exception'], stack trace discarded
SubscriberExecutionFailedException proxies StackTrace/Source from original exception (src/Headless.Messaging.Core/Internal/SubscriberExecutionFailedException.cs)
_ChangeMessageStateAsync is shared between publish/receive in SqlServer and PostgreSql — must split for receive-only ExceptionInfo column
SELECT * with ordinal readers in GetMessagesAsync — safe if ExceptionInfo is last column (reader stops at StatusName index 8)
MessageDetailDialog.vue has dead exception rendering code (isExceptionObject/buildContentKind) that never fires — content is Message JSON wrapper, not exception object
IDataStorage.StoreReceivedExceptionMessageAsync is public interface — adding default parameter is backward-compatible
Two failure paths: Path A (deserialization in IConsumerRegister:250) and Path B (subscriber execution in ISubscribeExecutor:175)
